methods.method_name: Bioinformatics Analysis of Capture Enrichment Data
methods.description: This method outlines the computational workflow applied to raw sequencing data from capture enrichment experiments, focusing on ancient DNA. It includes steps for quality control, read mapping, taxonomic classification, degradation analysis, and consensus sequence generation.

steps.description: Adapter sequences were removed and sequences were filtered using leeHom with the default ancient DNA settings (Renaud et al., 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Remove adapter sequences and filter reads using leeHom with ancient DNA settings
Remove duplicate reads from filtered sequences

steps.description: filtered reads were mapped against a database of 73 mammal mitogenomes (Appendix 1-table 4) using BWA v. 0.7.17 (Li and Durbin, 2009)
steps.searchable_summary: Map filtered reads against a mammal mitogenome database using BWA
inputs: Filtered reads with duplicates removed (from Step 2)

steps.description: were blastn-aligned against the complete NCBI Genbank nucleotide database (Benson et al., 2009;retrieved March 14 2022) with a maximum e-value of 0.01.
steps.searchable_summary: Align filtered reads against NCBI Genbank nucleotide database using blastn
inputs: Filtered reads with duplicates removed (from Step 2)

steps.description: All blast output files were processed using MEGAN Community Edition 6.19.8 using a weighted LCA algorithm (Huson et al., 2016).
steps.searchable_summary: Process blast output files for taxonomic assignment using MEGAN with weighted LCA algorithm

steps.description: aDNA degradation was examined using mapDamage 2.2.1 (Ginolhac et al., 2011) against a reference genome of M. primigenius (NC_007596.2).
steps.searchable_summary: Examine aDNA degradation patterns using mapDamage against a reference genome

steps.description: The reads blast-assigned to M. primigenius were mapped to the complete M. primigenius reference mitogenome to produce consensus sequences using Geneious Prime 2023.0.1 (Kearse et al., 2012).
steps.searchable_summary: Map reads assigned to M. primigenius to the M. primigenius reference mitogenome using Geneious Prime
inputs: Reads assigned to M. primigenius (from Step 5)
outputs: Mapped reads (alignments specific to M. primigenius mitogenome)

steps.description: The reads blast-assigned to M. primigenius were mapped to the complete M. primigenius reference mitogenome to produce consensus sequences using Geneious Prime 2023.0.1 (Kearse et al., 2012).
steps.searchable_summary: Produce consensus sequences from mapped reads using Geneious Prime
